prompt: A Unified Admissions & Student Services Management System that includes end-to-end configuration of admissions plans, consultation handling, and appointment booking for an education provider that operates fully on-premises. Primary roles are Applicant (submits questions and requests appointments), Admissions Advisor (responds to consultations and manages bookings), Department Manager (routes work and approves plan changes), Data Steward (governs master data), and System Administrator (security and system settings). In the Vue.js web UI, applicants browse published admissions plans by year and intake batch (for example, Fall 2026), view program tracks and capacity notes, and submit consultation tickets with text and optional images (JPEG/PNG up to 5 MB each, max 3 attachments). Tickets require a category tag and priority (Normal/High); the interface confirms submission with a local ticket number and shows status changes in real time via periodic polling. Advisors work from a triage inbox that supports routing by department and tag, with SLA timers displayed: High priority tickets must receive a first response within 2 business hours, Normal within 1 business day; overdue tickets are flagged and can be reassigned by managers with a required reason. Booking screens support placing, rescheduling, and canceling appointments for interviews or advising sessions; users see immediate validation feedback for policy rules such as “reschedule allowed up to 24 hours before start,” “cancel allowed up to 12 hours before start,” and “no-show after 10 minutes past start marks the slot consumed.” Managers configure annual/batch admissions plans and program/track options through a draft-review-publish workflow; published plans become read-only and are traceable to the approving manager and effective date, with version comparisons available in the UI.The backend uses Laravel to implement REST-style APIs consumed by the Vue.js frontend, enforcing server-side validation, workflow states, and transactional consistency, with MySQL as the system of record. Admissions plans are versioned entities with immutable published snapshots; post-publication edits require a new version that re-enters submit-review-publish, and every state transition writes an append-only audit entry with actor, timestamp, IP, and before/after hashes. Consultation tickets store the full conversation transcript and attachment metadata; image files are stored locally on disk with SHA-256 fingerprints and strict MIME/type checks to prevent tampering, and quality-review sampling automatically selects 5% of closed tickets per advisor per week for manager review with a lock to prevent retroactive edits. Booking concurrency is protected using database-backed distributed locks plus inventory pre-deduction on time slots, guaranteeing idempotency by requiring a client-generated request key valid for 10 minutes; duplicate submissions return the original booking result and never oversell capacity. Master data governance covers organizations, personnel, positions, and course categories with data dictionaries and coding rules (for example, ORG-000123), soft delete, full version history, and merge workflows for duplicates detected by normalized name + date-of-birth or employee ID, with merge requiring steward approval. Data quality metrics (completeness, consistency, uniqueness, timeliness) are computed nightly and stored for trend reporting. Security includes RBAC down to section/content level, admin MFA using offline TOTP, API authentication via signed session tokens, rate limiting and CAPTCHA after 5 failed logins in 15 minutes, content integrity checks on published plan artifacts, and detailed operation logs; sensitive fields (DOB, IDs) are encrypted at rest and masked in the UI based on role, ensuring the entire system remains fully functional without any internet dependency.
